Well, why do I want testimonials about a book in the middle of a testimonial page that is about getting me clients? Well, because one of those testimonials is from Joan Rivers and the other one is from at the time a hugely successful infomercial, Perfect Smile. And I didn't have testimonials from either one of them about me. I only had testimonials from them about the book. And they are more important than what the testimonial was about. The Joan Rivers thing always caused, you know, Joan, tell me about Joan. Well, I don't care what they want to hear about as long as they want to hear something because now we're in dialogue. And the Perfect Smile thing, because I got a lot of business out of the infomercial industry, that was extremely useful. Now the other thing to think about is those testimonials exist because I had a book and went to get cover blurbs for the book. The Perfect Smile one is actually a secondary favor arranged by Greg Ranker or Kathy Ranker, as a matter of fact. But without the book, I couldn't have got them. Once I got them for the book, I could keep using them in all sorts of places. I gave you my primary testimonial book that I used for a long time. I gave you pages of that in there too. It's actually published like this. So it's just testimonials, you know, in a book form. Called your attention to a few of them. I got the Brenda Dillard one in there for no other reason than I really liked it. I mean, I just, I didn't write any of these, by the way. I just really thought it was, it's still my favorite testimonial of all of the ones that I've had. This is in there for two reasons. One, because it's a testimonial, you know, with a story. See, without Irish immigrant in front of $8.40 invested in a sales letter, produces $26,000, et cetera, it's not nearly as interesting a story as when you stick Irish immigrant in front of it. And furthermore, it allows me to show the newsletter in my book of testimonials, which there's obviously, there's a whole bunch of reasons, you know, to do that. This one is in there purely because it's Hewlett Packard. And I don't have a lot of big company stuff, so it was good to have a Hewlett Packard one. In some cases, I reprinted the whole letters. This one's in there again for the same reason it's Hewlett Packard. It's B2B. And I have less business to business than I have anything else. So that's about as business to business as you can get. This one's in there because it's a cool attention-getting picture, and I like the picture. So she sent me this picture of herself. It's a baby in a bikini on two elephants. How many of you have ever been signed a testimonial with a letter of a baby in a bikini standing on two elephants? That's probably nobody but me, right? Do you think? This is in there. Why is this in there? Oh, this is in there because this is now another one of these personal ones. This is a booze problem related one. And so it's actually in there, has disclosure and encouragement to connect on that level. This is here because it's Australia. And having clients from distant lands, you want to remember that, having clients from distant lands is still extremely exciting and interesting and impressive to people. You would think you still had to go over there on the Queen Mary and take three months getting there the way people react. You can actually still get a gatekeeper to put somebody on the phone by adding the words calling long distance. Now you would think that would have gone away, but that still works. You want to remember that by the way if you're trying to get somebody on the phone. It's better to say this is Dan Kennedy calling for Dr. Altadonna long distance than this is Dan Kennedy calling for Dr. Altadonna. For some reason that still works. It creates some kind of little sense of urgency to go get them. Having a client from a distant place is still interesting to people. The Bridget Campbell one is in there for the same reason that the Brenda Dillon one is in there. I always liked it.
